title: Assistant Manager, IT (2 year contract) - hybrid arrangement
description:
You will be responsible for the end‑to‑end planning, execution,governance, and successful delivery of IT and digital transformationinitiatives. Your role ensures that project objectives, operationalrequirements, and governance standards are consistently achieved, while fostering alignment across internal departments, external vendors, senior leadership, and key stakeholders. Success in this position requires the ability to bridge business and technology by collaborating effectively with technical teams, translating business needs into actionable solutions, and guiding implementation without requiring deep developer expertise.


Job Responsibilities


Project Planning & Governance

  • Lead end‑to‑end project lifecycle in accordance with Project Management Guidelines.
  • Prepare and maintain all mandatory artefacts: Project Charter, Business Case, RFP & Vendor Score Sheets, Project Plan & Timeline, Risk & Control registers, Testing documentation, Project Closure File.
  • Ensure mandatory approval gates are met with clear documentation for risk, procurement, and senior management reviews.


Stakeholder & Cross‑Functional Coordination

  • Engage extensively with benefiting departments (operations, product owners, clinical teams, finance, HR, procurement).
  • Collect business requirements, validate process flows, and ensure operational alignment.
  • Manage complex stakeholder networks including senior management and external vendors.


Vendor & Project Team Management

  • Review vendor proposals, scope, timelines, and resourcing.
  • Coordinate solution design, development, configuration, testing, and deployment.
  • Oversee vendor performance and contract adherence.
  • Provide project leadership to IT team members and cross‑functional project contributors.


Execution & Delivery

  • Coordinate system design discussions, UAT planning, test scenario reviews, defect resolution and retesting.
  • Manage go‑live activities, technical cutover, post‑go‑live review and handover to operations.
  • Track project risks, issues, dependencies, and mitigation strategies.


Reporting & Compliance

  • Ensure auditability and traceability for all project artefacts.
  • Provide updates on progress, budget, risks, and mitigation.
  • Ensure compliance with governance, procurement, and finance requirements.
  • Timely KPI reporting to Grant providers.


Requirements

  • Bachelor Degree in IT, Computer Science, Engineering, or relevant field.
  • Min 6 yrs of experience, with at least 3 years in a managerial capacity.
  • Strong planning, documentation, and project governance capabilities.
  • Exp in managing vendors and system implementation projects (preferred: case management systems, CRM, finance and HR systems).
  • Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills.
  • Able to work under pressure and manage multiple parallel workstreams.
  • Exp in Government Grant application and KPI reporting.
  • Exp in Social Service Agencies will be an advantage.
  • Exp in Project management methodologies (Waterfall, Agile hybrid).
  • Strong understanding of full stack web development concepts (front-end, back-end, databases, APIs) sufficient to collaborate with technical teams and translate requirements, without needing to code at a developer level.
  • Working knowledge of Salesforce CRM capabilities, workflows, and integration points, with the ability to gather requirements and liaise with developers/administrators rather than perform hands-on configuration.
  • Familiarity with process automation using low‑code/no‑code platforms (e.g., Power Automate, n8n, Salesforce Flow), with the ability to design and optimize workflows in collaboration with technical teams.
  • Strong analytical, problem‑solving, communication skills and decision‑making skills.
  • Ability to lead cross‑functional teams.

postedCompany.description:
(1) Our Origins


Dementia Singapore (DSG) was formed in 1990 as Alzheimer’s Disease Association to better serve Singapore’s growing dementia community, increase awareness about dementia, and reduce the stigma surrounding the condition.


As Singapore’s leading Social Service Agency (SSA) in specialised dementia care, Dementia Singapore aims to advocate for the needs of people living with dementia and their families; empower the community through capability-building, knowledge and consultancy; and deliver quality, person-centred care innovations.


For more information, visit https://dementia.org.sg/



(2) Our Employee Value Proposition


Because We Care


“We are professionals committed to enhancing dementia care in Singapore.


We support each other in our common pursuit to inspire society to regard and respect persons living with dementia as individuals to lead purposeful and meaningful lives and create a more compassionate society.


Communication, openness and innovation are key traits within our family. We listen to each other’s feedback and suggestions, and work together collaboratively to make DSG a better place to work in.


We care for the well-being of our team so that we can provide the highest standard of care to our clients. This includes providing for career growth for all employees of DSG, while catering for good work-life harmony to allow for personal growth and development.”



(3) Hybrid Work Arrangement


As part of our Work-Life Harmony strategy, we adopted a hybrid work arrangement (subject to the nature of the job).


Flexi-Time


We have Staggered Timing for staff to choose their starting and ending work hours.

At DSG, we believe that achieving work-life balance will allow every valued staff of the DSG family to satisfy their professional and personal goals.



(4) Diversity, Equity & Inclusion at the Heart of Our Culture

At Dementia Singapore, diversity, equity, and inclusion are not add-ons, they are integral to who we are and how we work. We embrace a culture where every team member feels valued, respected, and empowered to contribute meaningfully. 


We are committed to providing equal opportunities for career development, and we believe that diverse perspectives lead to better outcomes for the people we serve.
postedCompany.name: DEMENTIA SINGAPORE LTD.
